topic: Someone discovers their teenager has a secret social media account
raw_output:
[story 1]

The phone lay on the kitchen counter, a silent, obsidian rectangle mocking me. I watched Leo across the room, his posture rigid as he scrolled through something intensely private. His thumb moved with a practiced swiftness, a slight tension around his jaw that I’d learned to recognize instantly. My stomach tightened, a cold knot forming beneath my ribs. I’d been searching for his misplaced charging cable, but my focus kept snagging on the screen. When he finally locked the app, he didn't look up, just a brief, almost imperceptible shrug. The silence in the house suddenly felt thick, charged with unspoken things. I pushed myself up, the scrape of the chair legs against the hardwood sounding deafeningly loud. "Everything alright, sweetie?" My voice came out sharper than I intended, a little too high. He flinched, a quick, jerky movement, and mumbled something about homework, his eyes darting toward the hallway as if expecting an ambush.

[story 2]

I found the login details tucked inside an old, forgotten notebook—a strange, almost absurd discovery. The air in my bedroom felt suddenly thin, the light from the window too harsh. Every sound seemed amplified: the hum of the refrigerator, the distant traffic, even my own shallow breathing. I stared at the screen, the profile picture a carefully curated mask of carefree indifference. It wasn't just the secret; it was the sheer, hidden world he was constructing behind the veneer of his public persona. A tremor ran through my hand as I navigated the settings, my fingers clumsy with sudden anxiety. I felt a pressure building behind my eyes, a desperate need to know, to understand the landscape he inhabited that I was completely excluded from. When he walked in, whistling tunelessly, I quickly minimized the window, my heart hammering against my ribs like a trapped bird. "Just looking at pictures," I managed, the words tasting like ash.

[story 3]

It started with an unlocked browser tab, a fleeting glimpse of a username I didn't recognize. My pulse quickened, a frantic drumming against my eardrums. I moved toward his room slowly, deliberately, trying to keep my footsteps soft on the carpet. The atmosphere in the hallway seemed to vibrate; everything felt too loud, too exposed. I saw the device glowing faintly under his desk, and a wave of apprehension washed over me, heavy and suffocating. I hesitated at the threshold, my palms slick with sudden perspiration. I didn't want to intrude, but the urge to confront this hidden layer of his life was overwhelming. When he finally emerged, looking particularly withdrawn, I kept my expression neutral, forcing a calm I absolutely did not feel. "I just noticed something," I said, the statement hanging between us, heavy with unspoken accusation and dawning realization.
